content_preview.title: Stegra Lands 1.4 Billion Euros From a Wallenberg-Led Consortium to Finish the World's First Large-Scale Green Steel Plant
content_preview.summary: The Swedish startup secured 1.4 billion euros to complete its hydrogen-based Boden plant, a rare bright spot as other European green steel projects stall.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Swedish green steel startup Stegra has agreed in principle on 1.4 billion euros in new financing from a combination of new and existing investors, according to the company's [press release](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/stegra-has-agreed-in-principle-on-a-1-4-billion-financing-round-led-by-a-wallenberg-investments-consortium-302741384.html). The money is earmarked to complete construction of its large-scale green steel plant in Boden, Sweden, a project the company is buil...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 727-word News piece with clear sections (Overview, What We Know, The Plant, Against the Grain, What We Don't Know). Technical depth on the hydrogen direct-reduction process is appropriate and accurately rendered. The 'What We Don't Know' section is exemplary: it explicitly flags that the round is an agreement in principle subject to credit approvals, that no precise first-steel date was given, and that closing slated for June 2026 will determine the construction ramp.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 source snapshots read from disk (gunzipped, status 200, no archive fallback). source-0 (PR Newswire / Stegra release): confirms 'agreed in principle' on EUR 1.4 billion led by a Wallenberg Investments-consortium, consortium consists of Temasek and IMAS, Wallenberg taking 'a leading position', Altor 'second largest owner post-closing', Hy24 and Just Climate, scope expansions (insourcing of selected infrastructure components, increased project costs, prudent financial buffer), signing 'end of April' / closing 'during June 2026', Leif Johansson to be nominated chair succeeding Shaun Kingsbury — all verbatim. source-1 (IPE Real Assets): CEO quote 'This financing reflects the strong conviction in Stegra's business model among new and existing investors, as well as lenders' is verbatim, correctly attributed to 'Henrik Henriksson, Stegra's CEO'; Kingsbury proposed to remain on the board, Hakan Buskhe (of Wallenberg Investments) and Paal Weberg (of Altor) nominations confirmed. source-2 (Stegra Boden plant): 5 million tonnes by 2030, 700-megawatt hydrogen plant, hydrogen 'made from water and renewable electricity', up to 95% CO2 reduction, direct reduction process — all confirmed. source-3 (EUROMETAL): 'EUR 1.4 billion ($1.65 billion)', temporary construction break late last year, first steel production initially expected this year, new capital subject to credit approvals enabling completion and commissioning — verbatim. source-4 (EUROMETAL green steel markets 2026): ArcelorMittal canceled DRI modules even with government funding, Thyssenkrupp put hydrogen tender on hold due to elevated prices but committed to Duisburg, SSAB postponed from end of 2028 to end of 2029 due to power-grid modernization delays — all verbatim. source-5 (Stegra/Mercedes-Benz): binding agreement covering about 50,000 tonnes per year produced at Boden, and 'formerly known as H2 Green Steel' framing — confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific traces to a cited source. Load-bearing facts are corroborated across multiple sources: the EUR 1.4 billion figure appears in source-0 (Stegra), source-1 (IPE) and source-3 (EUROMETAL); the Wallenberg-led consortium appears in source-0 and source-1. The EUR 1.4B is correctly framed as an agreement in principle subject to credit approvals, both in the Overview and explicitly in 'What We Don't Know'. Timing is handled honestly: the article reports the company's stated 'end of April' signing and 'June 2026' closing without misrepresenting the publication date. The dropped unsourced specifics — a 'Lulea' place name for Stegra and a '2021' founding date — are genuinely ABSENT from the body (Rule 2 satisfied); note 'Lulea' appears in source-4 only in reference to SSAB's separate project, and the article correctly does not attribute it to Stegra. The 'Hakan' diacritic (a-ring, U+00E5) is preserved verbatim in 'Hakan Buskhe', matching the source (Rule 7 satisfied).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The 'Against the Grain' framing (Stegra as a bright spot amid stalled European green steel) is supported by the EUROMETAL market survey and is presented as reported fact, not editorializing. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No existing article covers Stegra, green steel, or this financing round. The two nearest 2026-06 articles (a Pyrenees cave dig and a Luxembourg green hydrogen plant) are unrelated.
concerns: Three cited domains are off the source allowlist: stegra.com (primary company source, 2 URLs), eurometal.net (trade publication, 2 URLs), and realassets.ipe.com (IPE Real Assets). This is the single non-blocking warning from chief:review. The load-bearing facts (EUR 1.4B, Wallenberg as lead investor) are independently corroborated across these sources plus the PR Newswire release, so the off-allowlist status does not undermine verifiability — but it is documented in the corrections record per the v3.9.0 workflow.
recommendations: Maintainers may consider evaluating stegra.com (primary company), eurometal.net, and realassets.ipe.com for future allowlist inclusion if green steel / industrial-decarbonization coverage continues; not added in this review per scope.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong, accurate, and original News piece with every claim sourced and every quote verbatim. The only issue is three off-allowlist domains, which is honestly summarizable in a corrections note. Verdict: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
